marketState: neutral, leaning cautiously bullish medium-term on weather but short-term overbought
summary: Cooling demand is running well above normal both this week (+12% vs 5y, near top of range) and in the 8-16 day window (+20.1%, rangePos 0.97), the single strongest input in the data and an argument for continued firmness into summer. Seasonality agrees directionally (price above median YTD, 63% odds higher in 4 weeks) but its own reliability score is rated Low, so it should not be leaned on heavily alone. Front price ($3.245) is already running $0.32 above the current fair-value estimate ($2.929, SLIGHTLY OVER), and that premium only fully normalizes by roughly day 21 as fair value drifts up to about $3.10 - so the market has already partly priced the weather story. Storage is a genuine counterweight: a +13 Bcf bearish build surprise and a +175 Bcf cushion above the 5-year average signal ample supply, while managed money is net short at just the 18th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded positioning that adds squeeze risk on further heat. Short-term technicals (5m/15m RSI in the high 70s-80s, price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h, 24h demand trend already turning down) show the rally is stretched and due to cool off over the next few sessions even if the medium-term direction stays up.

longTerm.entry: $2.95-$3.10 (buy a pullback toward the fair-value zone)
longTerm.rationale: Both weather windows (1-7D +12% vs 5y, 8-16D +20.1% vs 5y and near the top of their historical range) point to sustained above-normal cooling demand, and seasonality's 4-week up-probability (63%) plus positive vsMedian tilt add confirmation, though seasonality reliability is flagged Low. Fair value's own band rises from $2.929 today toward $3.104 by day 21, so a pullback entry closer to today's fair value offers better risk/reward than chasing the current $0.32 premium.
longTerm.invalidation: Demand_8_16D pctVs5y and rangePos roll over toward/below normal (heat forecast breaks), or storage surprises keep printing bearish and price fails to hold above the fair-value band low near $2.75-2.90.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is already $0.32 above fair value (SLIGHTLY OVER) and intraday technicals are stretched - 5m RSI 83.8, 15m RSI 74.1, price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h - while the 1d MACD is negative and the 1w trend is bearish. The 24h weather trend also just turned down (-16.7 demand), and this week's storage build beat forecast (bearish surprise), reinforcing near-term mean reversion back toward the fair-value zone around $2.93-$3.05.
shortTerm.invalidation: 5m/15m RSI unwinds without price giving back the recent gains (no mean reversion), or weather demand re-accelerates and price breaks and holds above $3.36.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand well above 5y normal both near-term (+12%) and 8-16 days out (+20.1%, near max of range) - strongest bullish input
Seasonality tilts bullish (63% odds higher in 4wks, +3.3pp vs median) but reliability is rated Low (51pp spread)
Storage at 2,922 Bcf, +175 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish +13 Bcf build surprise - supply cushion caps upside
Managed money net short at just the 18th percentile of 5y range - crowded short positioning raises squeeze risk on further heat
Front price already $0.32 (SLIGHTLY OVER) above fair value with short-term technicals (5m/15m RSI >74, above upper Bollinger) overbought
risks: Weather can reverse quickly (24h demand already down 16.7) - a cooling forecast would remove the main bullish pillar for both trades
Storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y) could keep pressuring price if injections keep beating forecast into shoulder season
Crowded short COT positioning plus overbought short-term technicals creates two-sided event risk - a squeeze higher on sustained heat, or a sharp reversal lower if RSI/Bollinger normalize
